english: mčog (mchog) a most perfect vessel of religion (most susceptible of etc.) Thgy.; snod ma yin (snod ma yin) insusceptible of religion Thgy., Tar. — 2. in metaphysics: pýi-snód (phyi snod) the external world, or rather inanimate nature, pýi-snód-kyi ꜧig-rtén (phyi snod kyi 'jig rten) Glr. and elsewh. frq., opp. to naṅ-bčúd (nang bcud), viz. the sentient beings composing it; so Mil.; Sch.: matter and spirit. — II. v. snád-pa (snad pa).

pf. and fut. bsnan (bsnan), 1. to add, superadd, increase, augment, *la nán-c̀e* (la nan ce) W. to add to the wages, to raise the wages; *ja tsá-big nan sal* (ja tsha big nan sal) W. please give me some more tea! ynyis bsnán-te (gnyis bsnan te) two being added to them, (their number) increasing by two Mil.; máṅ-du snón-pa (mang du snon pa) to augment by a great number frq. — nón-ka (non ka), or nón-k̇a (non kha) W., increase, growth, augmentation, and in a special sense: agio, premium; snón-ma (snon ma), bsnán-ma (bsnan ma), id.; *puṅ-nóṅ* (phung nong) W., *gyab-nóṅ* (rgyab nong) C., dmag - tsógs snón - ma (dmag tshogs snon ma) reinforcements, auxiliary troops. — 2. to add up, sum up Wdk.
(spelling?) curious, inquisitive, *nob-zóg c̀ó-c̀e* (nob zog co ce) W. to pry into, to ferret.

I. also snúm-pa (snum pa), pf. bsnums (bsnums), fut. bsnum (bsnum), imp snum(s) (snum(s)); and snám-pa (snam pa), pf. bsnams (bsnams), fut. bsnam (bsnam), imp. snom(s) (snom(s)), 1. to smell, to perceive by the nose (cf. mnám-pa (mnam pa)), snas drí-rnams bsnáms-pa (snas dri rnams bsnams pa) to perceive scents by the nose Stg.; *da num* (da num) W. there, smell at that! *zi núm-te ḍúl-c̀e* (zi num te dul ce) W. to go about smelling and prying; *ṅa c̀iaṅ mi num* (nga ci ang mi num) W. I do not smell any thing. — 2. to grope, *mụn-nag-la nom-nẹ čin = nag-zúg-la nóm-žiṅ soṅ* (mun nag la nom ne chin = nag zug la nom zhing song) C., v. nag-zúg (nag zug). II. pf. bsnams (bsnams), fut. bsnam (bsnam), W. *nám-c̀e* (nam ce), resp. for lén-pa (len pa), ꜧdzín-pa ('dzin pa), tógs-pa (thogs pa), čáṅ-ba (chang ba), to take, relics from a sepulchre Glr.; to seize, to take up, the alms-bowl Dzl.; to hold, a stick Mil.; to put on, a sacred garment; *nam yin - na* (nam yin na) W. would you please (to take), would you like (to have a cup of tea etc.)?
pf. and fut. bsnor (bsnor), to confound, mingle, mix, disturb Cs.

pf. and fut. bsnol (bsnol), 1. to unite, join, put together, fit together, e.g. bricks or stones in building W.; Cs. to adjust; Sch.: to mend holes in stockings, to darn; to cross one's hands, bráṅ-k̇ar (brang khar), resp. túgs-k̇ar (thugs khar), on the breast Thgr. and elsewh. frq.; ꜧtám snól-ba ('tham snol ba) to put together, to embrace Cs.; ltá-snol-ba (lta snol ba) to look at each other, ꜧó-snol-ba ('o snol ba) to kiss each other, 'and thus frq. denoting reciprocity' Cs. (though not to my knowledge). — 2. to wrestle, scuffle, fight, of boys, dogs frq., also Mil.; stag snól-ba (stag snol ba) a fighting tiger that rushes upon the enemy Ma.; to contend with, fight against, subdue, me (me), a fire Tar.
the names of two of the lunar mansions, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar).
Lexx. = pŕed (phred); Cs. sloping, oblique; Sch.: confusedly, pellmell; Cs. also mediocrity.

1. to devise, contrive, to take care, to be concerned about, to strive for or after, ... žes yčig-tu brnág-pas (zhes gcig tu brnag pas) striving only after (that one thing) Tar.; as sbst. brnág-pa čoṅs (brnag pa chongs) keep (it) well in your mind, pay all attention (to it)! c. genit., cf. brnán-pa (brnan pa). — 2. Lex. = bzód-pa (bzod pa), to suffer, to endure; brnag-dka (brnag dka) intolerable, insupportable Lex. — 3. Cs.: to be full of corrupt matter.

1. Cs. to attend, to look on attentively, ꜧbri-klóg brnán-pa ('bri klog brnan pa) to attend while a person is reading or writing. — 2. Sch.: 'to be desirous of, to long for, čós-la (chos la) for religious instruction, ltó-la (lto la) for food'. With the first signification agrees a quotation in Zam.: nán-tan-brnan (nan tan brnan), with the second the word *zá-nan-c̀an* (za nan can) W., = zá-brnab-c̀an (za brnab can).
Cs.: covetousness, selfishness; Thgy.: bdág-gi-la brnáb-sems (bdag gi la brnab sems) predilection for one's own things, yžán-gyi-la brnáb-sems (gzhan gyi la brnab sems) desire for things
